observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a.	Comply with the guidelines and procedures on granting funds to NGOs/POs, private organizations/associations or corporations as provided under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007; and

b.	Stop giving donation/contributions or released of fund to private organizations except for activities pursuant to the mandate of the Municipality, as it is considered an irregular expense under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

c.	Make an adjusting entry to record Due from NGOs/POs, a receivable account, in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: They are still securing some documents from concerned NGO/PO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the:

a.    Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer or other authorized AO to prepare Report of Disbursements on each cash advance for payroll and ensure that cash advances are disbursed and liquidated within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular 97-005 dated February 10, 1997 and COA Circular No. 2012-001;

b.    Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure the Report of Disbursements are supported with paid payrolls and other supporting documents as stated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit to the Accounting Office for recording in the books; and

c.    Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the Report of Disbursements with complete supporting documents within 10 days to the State Auditors for evaluation and audit in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

a.    Always require the Municipal Engineer to provide the State Auditors with a list of ongoing 20% DF and MDRRM projects for proper monitoring on the implementation; and

b.    Inform COA within 10 days after the awarding of the contract of an infrastructure project, in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that the Management: 

Fill up the Municipal Government Department Head I (Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer V) responsible for overall management of LDRRM Office, if appropriate.  Otherwise, abolish the position and create more lower positions in the LDRRMO, as the LDRRM Officer III is enough to head the LDRRMO; and

Create one more mandatory position to complete the three staff requirement in the LDRRM Office responsible for either training, operations and research and fill up the same in compliance with the guidelines on creation of LDRRM Office in the municipality.
observations.reason:
The position was not filled-up
The finding was first raised in CY 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management: 

Fill up the Municipal Government Department Head I (Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer V) responsible for overall management of LDRRM Office, if appropriate.  Otherwise, abolish the position and create more lower positions in the LDRRMO, as the LDRRM Officer III is enough to head the LDRRMO; and

Create one more mandatory position to complete the three staff requirement in the LDRRM Office responsible for either training, operations and research and fill up the same in compliance with the guidelines on creation of LDRRM Office in the municipality.
observations.reason:
The position was not created. 
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6/Page 44 of this year’s AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant obtain complete documentation of long outstanding cash advances to support the legal claim of the Municipality from the concerned officers and employees. When deemed necessary and on valid grounds, such as for deceased accountable officers, the Municipal Accountant request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with the Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason:
The required reports were not submitted to COA
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3/Page 39 of this year’s AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management cause the immediate settlement of disallowances pertaining to PEI CY 2010. Management should likewise follow up the status of appeal on disallowed PEI and monetization benefits for CY 2009 with COA Regional Office No. VI.
observations.reason:
Partial settlements were made in CY 2018 to 2024
The finding was first raised in CY 2015 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare a certified computation of PS Cap applicable during the year to determine actual excess in the 45%/55% limit on personal services expenditures and determine whether PS Cost did not exceed PS Cap, otherwise, the amount expended for the PEI should be refunded by the municipal officials and employees who were recipients of the benefit.
observations.reason:
Partial settlements were made as of Dec. 31, 2024
The finding was first raised in CY 2009 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should convene the members of the Local Water Council to formulate specific rules and regulations on the operations of the water system to ensure that income from water system collections is fully realized to augment payment of loan amortization with the Land Bank of the Philippines.
observations.reason:
Specific rules and regulations relative to the operations of the water system were not yet finalized. The LGU is planning to create local water district of San Remigio
The finding was first raised in CY 2007 AAR

observations.recommendation: Municipal Mayor ensure that concerned officials make a consistent representation with the ICFAU pertaining to the status of the liquidation documents and the subsequent issuance Credit Advice.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

Discontinue the practice of designating job order personnel as collecting officers of the Municipality;   and

In the event these job order personnel continue performing revenue collection functions, securer their fidelity bonds pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 2-2019.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:

Municipal GSO prepare separate property cards on all classes of PPE as prescribed in the MNGAS for LGUs; and

Municipal Accountant maintain separate ledger cards of all PPE accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the following:

On the unreturned funds after project completion, that Municipal Mayor coordinate with the source agencies and request for authority to transfer these unexpended balances to the General Fund of the Municipality. Otherwise, remit it to the National Treasury or return unexpended balance of completed PPAs to the source agency, whichever is appropriate; and 

On the unutilized/not fully implemented projects, that the Municipal Mayor seek technical assistance from the concerned National Government Agencies or apply effective methods and exhaust all possible avenues to address pending issues or settle all differences which have or could like impede the project implementation; otherwise, return the same to the Source Agency.

Stop the practice of describing the priority development projects in generic terms upon inclusion in its Annual Budget by requiring the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged against the 20 percent Development project/program description and specific implementation schedule.

Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sanggunian Bayan exercise the prerogative and give priority to the revision and updating of the 2017 Local financial needs of the Municipality; and 

Municipal Treasurer and all other concerned department heads ensure that the revised rate of fees and charges are just and reasonable taking into consideration the application of LFC Toolkit pursuant to DBM-DOF- JMC No. 2019-01.
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure the submission of the complete set of financial statements within the prescribed period as provided in Section 41 of P.D No. 1445.

Immediately correct the noted deficiencies and comply as well with the Notice to Correct Violations of the Bureau of Fire to ensure the safety of the occupants of the buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the requested ledgers, once available, to the Audit Team for evaluation. 

Ensure the timely submission of the necessary subsidiary ledgers.

Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to prepare separate property cards on all classes of PPE as prescribed in the MNGAS for LGUs.  

Municipal Accountant maintain separate ledger cards for all PPE accounts.
Stop the practice of designating Job-Order personnel as collecting officers of the Municipality.

Seek approval from the Sanggunian and LDRRMC pertaining to the purchase of these motor vehicles.  

Strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the proper utilization of the DRRMF as stated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to diligently check the purpose of disbursements to ensure correctness in the charging of expenses in relation to funding source and its specific appropriations.
Conduct regular monitoring of the implementation of the above-cited recommendations
Immediately act for the revision of its GFPS organizational structure in order to comply with the requirements set forth under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

Prioritize the registration, transfer and titling of these parcels of land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.
Immediately demand refund to those officials and/or department heads concerned for all meals and snacks disbursed by the Municipality during the year, if necessary.

Submit the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation by the Audit Team. 

Adhere to the pertinent provisions stated in P.D. No. 1445, the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 and DBM NBC No. 563 to avoid the incurrence of unconscionable or excessive expenditures which are disallowable in audit.

Procure the common-use supplies and equipment from the Cebu Regional Depot of PS-DBM in accordance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011, Administrative Order No. 17 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.   

Comply with the documentary requirements stated in GPPB Resolution No. 17-2020 for those CSE items which are not available in the PS-DBM at the time of procurement before pursuing the procurement from other sources.

Direct the BAC to submit the documentary requirements of these contracted suppliers in order to regularize these procurement transactions.  

Strictly comply with the provisions stated in the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 concerning the guidelines on the procurement of goods & services through Alternative Mode of Procurement particularly under Negotiated Procurement-SVP.

Make representation with the DSWD, the source agency, as to the disposition of the assistance received by the ineligible beneficiaries taking into consideration the circumstances upon the release of such assistance.  

We further recommended informing us of subsequent developments on the status of the refund of the remaining illegally received assistance, if any.

All concerned officials and employees are directed to comply with the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount of excess honoraria received.

Allowable maximum rate of honoraria amounting to ₱15,500.00 originally taken from the SALINTUBIG-DILG fund be returned and be now taken from the proceeds of the sale of bid documents and fees from contractor/supplier in the trust fund books but if there are no available funds, then the said amount will also be disallowed.
All concerned officials and employees are directed to comply with the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount of honoraria received.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accounting Office prepare an adjusting entry in the general fund books in order to correct and/or reclassify the transfer of DRRMF to its proper account.
Adjusting entries as suggested (General Fund Books):
Debit      Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds   xxx
Credit     Unspent DRRMF          						           xxx
Henceforth, the following suggested entry be complied in the succeeding years,
Debit      Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds   xxx
Credit     Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund               xxx
To record the current year unspent DRRM funds to the Trust Fund.
Debit      Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund      xxx
Credit     Cash in Bank-LCCA					                                  xxx
To record transfer of cash to Trust Fund account.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accounting Office prepare an adjusting entry in the General Fund books to correct and/or reclassify the transfer of completed PPE from trust fund to its appropriate account in compliance with the international accounting standards and Revised Chart of Accounts.
Suggested adjusting entry (General Fund Books):
Debit      Prior Period Adjustment			 xxx
Credit     Subsidy from National Government        		    					 xxx
To correct and recognize revenue from current year transactions
Henceforth, the following suggested entry be complied in the succeeding years,
General Fund books:
Debit      PPE Account/s      					     		 xxx
Credit     Subsidy/Transfer from National Government        				 xxx
To record transfer of PPE from trust fund to general fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant conduct verification, analysis and validation of the collectability of the long unliquidated cash advances, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the recognition of the impairment in accordance with the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS).
The Municipal Mayor shall file the request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) on the following accountable officers/employees in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016:
For the deceased accountable officers/employees:
Required documents:
a.1 Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
a.2   Death Certificate issued by Philippine Statistics Authority (formerly National Statistics Office)
For accountable officers/employees that are no longer connected with the LGU with unknown whereabouts:
Required documents:
b.1 Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
b.2     Certificate of no residence in the barangay of the municipality/city of last known address;
b.3      Certification by Legal Officer of the entity of no pending case relative to the account; and
nb.4      Certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accounting and Budget Offices transfer the unexpended DRRMF-MOOE balance as of December 31, 2024 to Trust Fund Account and ensure that only capital outlay appropriations will remain continuing in the General Fund books in compliance with Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the transactions using the appropriate accounts pursuant to the descriptions provided for in COA Circular Nos. 2015-009 and 2024-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the cash balances with the cashbook by incorporating in the monthly BRS the balances per cashbook. Thus, the reconciliation would include balances per bank, balances per books and balances per cashbook in order to check the status of the actual cash available per bank account.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that entries in the cashbook are complete and properly footed so as to give the exact actual cash available for disbursements and refrain from further issuing checks should the cash in bank balance be insufficient to avoid overdraft.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each bank account for easy monitoring of the cash availability and easier comparison with the records of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider constituting the Inventory Committee in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to spearhead the conduct of physical count of all types of inventories owned by the Municipality and prepare the duly certified RPCI for submission to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively and thereafter prepare the RSMI which shall then be submitted to the Audit Team for further evaluation.
We also recommended and Management, through the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant, agreed to consider the provisions of COA Circular No. 2024-006 on the monitoring of the semi-expendable properties through various records such as Semi-Expendable Property Cards, Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Cards and the issuance of the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slips for accountability purposes.
Lastly, we recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant as well as the GSO, agreed to maintain stock cards on all supplies and materials to monitor its receipts and issuances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the monthly BRS to properly identify the reconciling items and to draw adjusting/correcting entries for valid reconciling items to ensure the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts in the financial statements. Moreover, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed on the timely submission of the BRS to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended and Management agreed, through:
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO, to conduct regular reconciliation of the records, investigate discrepancies noted, and make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of PPE accounts; and
The GSO, refrain from submitting RPCPPE without indicating the required entries of the report and to subsequently perform the annual physical count of PPEs in accordance with provisions stated above.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineering Office agreed to identify each component of the road network system (road lot, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures, and other miscellaneous structures) and furnish the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant revise the estimated useful life of road networks in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management through:
The Municipal Accountant, submit timely the Comparative Notes to Financial Statements to the Audit Team for evaluation indicating the reconciliation of the data between the SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance; and
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer, ensure the submission of the complete set of financial statements within the prescribed period as provided in Section 41(2) of P.D. No. 1445.
The Municipal Accountant agreed with the recommendations and was able to submit the SCBAA after the exit conference.

We recommended that Management:
Submit/seek approval/authorization from the Sanggunian pertaining to the expenditures in the generic appropriation;
Through the Municipal Development Council, specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged against the 20% DF which shall include, among others, project location, specific project/program description and specific implementation schedule; and
Through the Municipal Accountant, ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements prior to payment.

We recommended that Management:
Through the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer, submit explanation on the insufficient cash back up for the 20% DF since the IRA, now the Notice of Tax Allocation (NTA) is downloaded to the Municipality monthly; and
Require the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer to monitor cash utilization and refrain from using cash balances intended for the development fund to other PPAs.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal DRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer, submit a justification on the noted deficiencies and henceforth ensure that the 30% Quick Response Fund in the LDRRMFIP is presented in lump-sum in compliance with Section 4.0 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to cause the revision of Executive Order (EO) No. 34, series of 2022 taking into consideration the compliance with the requirements for the establishment of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), including the GFPS Technical Working Group (TWG) and Secretariat, as set forth in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal officials/employees concerned agreed to:
Prepare the Agency Plans identifying the programs, and projects intended to address the concerns of the Senior Citizens and Persons with disability of the municipality for the year; and
Submit the respective Accomplishment Reports to the NCSC (for older person) and NCDA (for persons with disabilities).
We further recommended and Management agreed that copies of the duly reviewed and endorsed plans and reports be furnished to the Audit Team for evaluation.

We recommended and Management through the BAC Secretariat in coordination with the Municipal Engineer agreed to:
Submit the contracts with complete set of supporting documents within the prescribed period to facilitate the immediate conduct of the auditorial and technical review of the projects; and
For completed projects, submit the complete set of documents for technical inspection.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant to evaluate those transfers/ financial assistance to other LGUs with specific purposes and determine if Fund Utilization Reports were already submitted by the recipient LGUs, otherwise, require their submission prior to the release of subsequent financial assistance;
The Municipal Accountant to carefully review the transactions during the month and prepare subsidiary ledgers for each transfer with specific purpose for proper monitoring of its utilization; and
Execute a MOA between the source (municipality) and the implementing (recipient) barangays for every grant of financial assistance with specific purpose to properly define their respective responsibilities.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Accounting Office to comply with proper recording of supplies and materials as prescribed under Section 114 and 126 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a.	Instruct the Treasurer’s Office to prepare and submit regularly the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the State Auditors to validate the charge invoices issued by the suppliers against the gas slips issued by the municipal mayor or his authorized representative.

b.	Regulate issuances of gas slips within the budgetary limits for gasoline and lubricants.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a.	Comply with the guidelines and procedures in granting fund to NGOs/POs, private organizations/associations or corporations as provided under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007; and

b.	Stop giving donation or released of fund to private entities except for activities pursuant to the mandate of the Municipality, as it is considered an irregular expense under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

c.	Make an adjusting entry to record Due from NGOs/POs, a receivable account, in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a.	Reevaluate the payment of telephone allowance granted to its officials and employees as there are cheaper, more economical and reasonable options offered by telephone companies nowadays;

b.	Payments of telephone expenses shall be made on a reimbursement basis as there is no law authorizing the grant of the same on a monthly basis; and

c.	Ensure that payments are supported with complete documentary requirements as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned offices to comply with the guidelines and procedures on submission of perfected contracts and Purchase Orders together with its supporting documents to State Auditors within five (5) days in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.  

We also recommended and the Management agreed to get hold and study the guidelines of COA Circular No. 2021-006 dated September 6, 2021 (Guidelines on the use of Electronic Documents, Electronic Signatures, and Digital Signatures in Government Transactions), as one of the alternatives to the physical submission of accounts to COA.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

a.	Direct the Treasurer’s Office to assign personnel to be responsible for filing and submission of accounts and related reports to Accounting Office within the prescribed period; and

b.	Require the Accounting Office and other concerned offices and ensure compliance to COA rules and regulations on submission of vouchers, RCD and other accounts to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor call the attention of GAD Focal Point regarding the preparation of the required GAD reports and regular submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period regularly in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that:

Actual physical count of inventories be conducted annually preferably at year-end, and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories which shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials facilitate the closure of this account by returning the unexpended balance of ₱75,016.00 to the source agency.
Municipal Treasurer religiously and diligently comply with the provision of the LTOM, 2nd Edition, Book II, taking into emphasis the daily turnover of collections by all collectors.

Submit the disbursement vouchers and its attachments on the granting of cash advances for CF within seven working days from release of the check pursuant to COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
Municipal Treasurer to ensure the immediate compliance with the abovementioned guidelines on the proper disposal of these obsolete checks so as to prevent the possibility of misuse.

Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all unissued Accountable forms under the custody of their Office be always kept inside a locked waterproof cabinet or safe especially at the end of the business day.

Municipal Treasurer to:

Religiously and diligently comply with the provisions of Section 90.C.1 of the LTOM, 2nd Edition, Book II, including the recurring audit observation; and Forms and Annexes in order to resolve this recurring audit observation; and 

Make a periodic monitoring on the compliance by all the Collecting Officers with the above-stated provisions.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend  and Management agreed on the following:

On the non-return of unexpended balances of financial assistance, conduct further evaluation/verification of each  of these projects and thereafter, decide or consider the following courses of actions: 

Designate a focal person to keep complete record/information of the funds/project and closely monitor their status to ensure immediate projects implementation and compliance with existing regulations; and  

Request for an authority from the Source Agency to use these balances to apply the funds to other purpose, otherwise, immediately return the same.

On the non-utilization and non-implementation of projects, the concerned department heads seek technical  assistance from the Provincial Government of  Cebu to address issues on reasons why these funds remained unutilized/implemented;

Municipal Accountant conduct regular monitoring and analysis of this account to ensure that fund transfer are liquidated within the remained period or upon completion of the project.
Concerned officials to conduct an extensive and holistic planning both during project conceptualization and implementation to ensure timely completion of identified development projects.

Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials ensure to:

Release the retention money only after the issuance of the Certificate of final Acceptance, which shall be made after one year from project completion pursuant to Section 62.2 of the 2016 of R.A. No 9184;

Require a substitution of the retention money with irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amounts equivalent to the retention money substituted/released in instances wherein the retention money is released to the contractor prior to the issuance of a Certificate of Final Acceptance; and

Monitor the completion dates of infrastructure projects and ensure that the releases of its corresponding retention money are in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of R.A. No 9184.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials provide explanation as to why the unexpended balance of the current year’s DRRMF-QRF was not yet transferred to as a Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability-DRRMF accounts in its Trust Books as of December 31, 2023; and

Municipal Accountant Effect the necessary adjustments to properly transfer the unexpended portion of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer provide explanation as to allowing these disbursements to be charged against the 30 percent QRF despite the absence of a Local Sanggunian Resolution and Declaration of State of Calamity and provide the lacking documentary requirements if necessary. 

The LDRRM Officer ensure that Local Sanggunian Resolution and Declaration of   State of Calamity shall be secured prior to utilization of the 30 percent QRF of the LDRRMF.

The MDRRM officer, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, strictly comply with the Provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent issuances particularly on the propriety of charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF; and 

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer diligently check the purpose of disbursements to ensure correctness in the charging of expenses in relation to funding source and its specific appropriations.

GFPS to submit to the Audit Team the approved CY 2023 GAD AR in the form prescribed by PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA-JMC No, 2013-001, as amended and prospectively submit timely the GAD AR to the Audit Team which shall be within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

MCPC to: 

Provide appropriation by attribution or allocate separately the required one percent of the National Tax Allotment to be used for the strengthening and implementation of allowable programs, projects an activities of the MCPC which should be consistent with the approved Annual Work and Financial Plan, and

Prepare and submit to the Audit Team the CY 2023 accomplishment report as indicated in the State of Municipal Address (SOMA) and the State of Children Report duly received by the Provincial Inter-Agency Monitoring Task Force.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials to: 

Conduct investigation as to the reasons on the incurrence of delayed renewal of license to operate and authorization for both Birthing Centers I and II;

Thereafter, require the erring officials/employees concerned on the renewal process to refund the total amount of ₱6,000.00; and

Ensure that the License to operate and Certificate of Accreditation of the Birthing Centers are timely processed and secured to comply with the standards set by the DOH and avoid incurrence of any penalty/surcharge.

Provide justification as to why remedial measures were not undertaken prior to the procurement of reconditioned excavator and secure approval/authority from the DBM, if applicable/ necessary. 

Require the BAC to submit an explanation on the noted deficiency. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension or Disallowance, as the case may be, shall be issued on this transaction.

MDRRMO formulate and implement its comprehensive LDRRMP in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and conduct judicious planning and scheduling to ensure the implementation of its disaster prevention PPAs.  

Release and use the LDRRMF only for those identified PPAs in the LDRRMP.

Municipal Mayor immediately provide the Office of the Municipal Assessor the adequate personnel, facility and equipment support for the timely and regular conduct of tax mapping operations in compliance with Section 472(b)(5) of R.A. No. 7160 and Chapter II, Section 2 of the MRPAAO with the end view of making an updated TMCR, among other records.

Submit an explanation on the above-cited deficiencies and immediately initiate steps towards the implementation of the minimum organizational structure and staffing pattern in compliance with Section 472 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 5, Chapter I of the MRPAAO.

Instruct the former members of the GAD M&E Team to immediately conduct evaluation on the outcomes of GAD policies, programs and programs implemented during CYs 2019 to 2022, accomplish the GAD Evaluation Report in the form as prescribed in Annex H of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.  A copy of the GAD Evaluation Report be furnished to the Audit Team for further evaluation.

Appropriate funds towards the capability building of proper officials and employees to gender sensitivity training (GST), including the use of the Gender-Responsive LGU (GeRL) Assessment Tool, HGDG and the GMEF in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

GFPS immediately provide technical assistance to the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator on the maintenance of a GAD database in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

Ensure that such database is complete and sex-disaggregated in accordance with the data requirements as presented in Annex B of the JMC.

GFPS Execom immediately submit to the Audit Team a written justification on the noted deficiencies in its utilization of GAD Fund including the submission of Resolution No. 2022-280 and supporting documentation of actual results regarding the two GAD PPAs with total budgetary requirements of ₱444,100.00, for evaluation.  

GFPS Execom exercise their function with prudence to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD budget in accordance with the above-cited provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

Direct the Inventory Committee to follow the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 particularly on the assignment of property numbers and tagging of property stickers in order to facilitate effective and efficient verification of the LGU’s PPE as to existence, condition and accountability.
Municipal Accountant immediately submit to the Audit Team the subsidiary ledgers for all Biological Assets accounts totaling ₱36.872 million as of December 31, 2021.

Desist from adopting this practice of procuring goods and services through direct contracting and paying such by way of reimbursement to the concerned Municipal Officials and employees and instead effectively implement and conform its procurement activities as laid out in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). 

Strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check which are directly paid to the suppliers, contractors, dealers and creditors.

Submit explanation and/or legal basis on the deviation committed in the procurement of project in accordance with R.A. 9184.  

We further recommended Management to strictly observe the provisions in R.A. 10752 for those projects requiring acquisition of real properties.

Require the BAC to prepare the approved APP and PMR in the format prescribed and submit the same to the GPPB in accordance with Sections 7.3.5 and 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

Barangay Bagsakan Project of the Department of Agriculture be made as one of the priority projects of the Municipality so as not to waste the purchased equipment in the initial implementation and fulfill the objectives of this project.
There be continued monitoring of the BB Project to determine its feasibility since this is still in its initial implementation and various changes and problems are still to be encountered.

We recommended that the Management: 

Fill up the Municipal Government Department Head I (Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer V) responsible for overall management of LDRRM Office, if appropriate.  Otherwise, abolish the position and create more lower positions in the LDRRMO, as the LDRRM Officer III is enough to head the LDRRMO; and

Create one more mandatory position to complete the three staff requirement in the LDRRM Office responsible for either training, operations and research and fill up the same in compliance with the guidelines on creation of LDRRM Office in the municipality.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed on the following:
A. On the non-return of unexpended balances of financial assistance, conduct further evaluation/verification of each of these projects and thereafter, decide or consider the following courses of actions:
i. Designate a focal person to keep complete record/information of the funds/project and closely monitor their status to ensure immediate project implementation and compliance with existing regulations; and
ii. Request for an authority from the Source Agency to use these balances to apply the funds to other purpose, otherwise, immediately return the same.
B. On the non-utilization and non-implementation of projects, the concerned department heads seek technical assistance from the Provincial Government of Cebu to address issues on reasons why these funds remained unutilized/unimplemented;
C. Municipal Accountant conduct regular monitoring and analysis of this account to ensure that fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period or upon completion of the project.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
On the unreturned funds after project completion, that Municipal Mayor coordinate with the source agencies and request for authority to transfer these unexpended balances to the General Fund of the Municipality. Otherwise, remit it to the National Treasury or return the unexpended balance of completed PPAs to the source agency, whichever is appropriate; and
On the unutilized/not fully implemented projects, that the Municipal Mayor seek technical assistance from the concerned National Government Agencies or apply effective methods and exhaust all possible avenues to address pending issues or settle all differences which have or could like impede the project implementation; otherwise, return the same to the Source Agency.

We reiterated our recommendation and the concerned officials agreed to conduct an extensive and holistic planning both during project conceptualization and implementation to ensure timely completion of identified development projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of describing the priority development projects in generic terms upon inclusion in its Annual Budget by requiring the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged against the 20 per cent Development Fund which shall include, among others, project location, specific project/program description and specific implementation schedule.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials ensure to:
Release the retention money only after the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance, which shall be made after one year from project completion pursuant to Section 62.2 of the 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184;
Require a substitution of the retention money with irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amounts equivalent to the retention money substituted/released in instances wherein the retention money is released to the contractor prior to the issuance of a Certificate of Final Acceptance; and
Monitor the completion dates of infrastructure projects and ensure that the releases of its corresponding retention money are in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials provide explanation as to why the unexpended balance of the current year’s DRRMF- QRF was not yet transferred to a Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability-DRRMF account in its Trust Fund Books as of December 31, 2023; and
Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments to properly transfer the unexpended portion of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The MDRRM Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent issuances particularly on the propriety of charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF; and
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer diligently check the purpose of disbursements to ensure correctness in the charging of expenses in relation to funding source and its specific appropriations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan exercise the prerogative and give priority to the revision and updating of the 2017 Local Revenue Code pursuant to the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 to help meet the financial needs of the Municipality; and
Municipal Treasurer and all other concerned department heads ensure that the revised rate of fees and charges are just and reasonable taking into consideration the application of LFC Toolkit pursuant to DBM-DOF JMC No. 2019-01.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to submit to the Audit Team the approved CY 2023 GAD AR in the form prescribed by PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001, as amended and prospectively submit timely the GAD AR to the Audit Team which shall be within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

We recommended and the MCPC agreed:
Provide appropriation by attribution or allocate separately the required one per cent  of the National Tax Allotment to be used for the strengthening and implementation of allowable programs, projects and activities of the MCPC which should be consistent with the approved Annual Work and Financial Plan; and

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to ensure the submission of the complete set of financial statements within the prescribed period as provided in Section 41(2) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the unutilized fund from the Bayanihan Grant to Cities/Municipalities (BGCM) be fully utilized and implemented in the ensuing year exclusively for programs, projects, and activities allowed under the DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 in order to address COVID-19 emergencies in the Municipality. If found still feasible for implementation, utilize the fund for its purpose in the ensuing year, otherwise, realign the fund for projects that are procurement-and-implementation-ready in compliance with the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.  Henceforth, the identification of development projects shall be well planned considering all the factors affecting its timely procurement and implementation.

We recommended that the LGU establish its GAD Database in compliance with RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women and in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 in order to come up with the most accurate and complete GAD Plan that addresses the gender issues and concerns of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board convene regularly and cause the approval of the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality in compliance with Section 16 of Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for the necessary accounting entry for the completed public infrastructure projects of ₱12.001 million in the Trust Fund books so that assets of the Municipality be fairly presented in the financial statement.

observations.recommendation: Make representation with the DSWD, the source agency, as to the disposition of the assistance received by the ineligible beneficiaries taking into consideration the circumstances upon the release of such assistance.  We further recommended informing us of subsequent developments on the status of the refund of the remaining illegally received assistance, if any.
observations.managementAction:
The Management explained that a total of 126 beneficiaries are from Unconditional Cash Transfer (UCT)-Listahanan whom they have received their first tranche prior to DSWD Advisory 8  which did not took effect in the first tranche distribution since it was disbursed to the identified UCT-Listahanan prior to the assessment on the Socio-economic status of the regular UCT. 

A total of 58 SAP beneficiaries were recipient of the emergency subsidy from other Government Owned & Controlled Corporation such as the Social Security System (SSS) which was not coordinated with the LGU. These beneficiaries received first their SAP subsidy from the DSWD.  If only deduplication process has been set prior to the first tranche distribution, the LGU could at least delist or remove them from official list of DSWD. 

The remaining 42 beneficiaries were identified ineligible beneficiaries for the reason of double entry in other LGUs where they have previously resided or where they stayed during the lockdown.

Finally, the Management explained that sending out demand letters will not help easing their problem considering that COVID-19 pandemic and the new variant is still in threat.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation to make representation with the DSWD, source agency regarding the disposition on the release of assistance to ineligible beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management examine the nature of its continued possession of these motor vehicles and, if appropriate, immediately facilitate the transfer of the Certificate of Registration in the name of the Municipality in compliance with R.A. No. 4136.

We recommended and Management agreed that Municipal Accountant immediately submit to the Audit Team the subsidiary ledgers for all Biological Assets accounts totaling ₱36,872,265.22 as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the Management immediately implement the priority projects in order to provide the intended benefits due to its constituents and facilitate liquidation thereof.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant initiate the return of the unused funds to the source agencies so as to clear the books of the said liability and present a reliable balance of the account.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991 specifically Section 444 thereof which requires that the Local Chief Executive exercise general supervision and control over all programs, projects, services, and activities of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that Management submit explanation as to the non-compliance with the aforecited provisions including submission of documentary requirements, if any, to support the corresponding disbursements.  We further recommended that Management stop the practice of implementing generic or lump-sum appropriations on identified development projects unless further approved and authorized by the Sanggunian.  Lastly, we recommended that Management to require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged against the 20% Development Fund which shall include, among others, project location, specific project/program description and specific implementation schedule.
We recommended that the Management immediately submit the lacking documentary requirements supporting the final payment on the acquisition of lot.

We recommended that the MDRRMO, henceforth, formulate and implement its comprehensive LDRRMP in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and conduct judicious planning and scheduling to ensure implementation of its disaster prevention PPAs.  We further recommended that Management release and use the LDRRMF only for those identified PPAs in the LDRRMP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the LDRRM Officer incorporate in the LDRMMFIP the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRM fund of the previous years, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent issuances particularly on the propriety of charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF.  We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to diligently check the purpose of disbursements to ensure correctness in the charging of expenses in relation to funding source and its specific appropriations.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation/justification for the incurrence of expenditures without or with insufficient appropriations for further audit evaluation. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer henceforth closely monitor the utilization of funds against the Municipal Budget for each item of expenditure.

We recommended that Management immediately demand refund to those officials and/or department heads concerned for all meals and snacks disbursed by the Municipality during the year, if necessary.  We also recommended that Management discontinue the practice of charging snacks and beverages using the Municipal funds and henceforth, comply with the provisions of Section 343 of R.A. No. 7160 to prevent the incurrence of irregular and unnecessary expenditures and wastage of government funds.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation by the Audit Team. Further, we recommended that Management adhere to the pertinent provisions stated in P.D. No. 1445, the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 and DBM NBC No. 563 to avoid the incurrence of unconscionable or excessive expenditures which are disallowable in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine and provide the adjusting entries representing the total amount of grants and donations extended to Barangays that were erroneously recorded in the ‘Donations’ expense account instead of under “Subsidy to Other LGUs” account. We further recommended Management to provide explanation on the release of financial assistance in excess of its appropriations.  Lastly, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance institutionalizing the release of financial assistance to indigents putting in mechanism such as qualification of indigents, amount of assistance vis-à-vis kinds of disease/diagnosis, reportorial requirements and the like.

We recommended that the Management procure the common-use supplies and equipment from the Cebu Regional Depot of PS-DBM in accordance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011, Administrative Order No. 17 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.   We also recommended that Management comply with the documentary requirements stated in GPPB Resolution No. 17-2020 for those CSE items which are not available in the PS-DBM at the time of procurement before pursuing the procurement from other sources.

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the documentary requirements of these contracted suppliers in order to regularize these procurement transactions.  Henceforth, we recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions stated in the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 concerning the guidelines on the procurement of goods & services through Alternative Mode of Procurement particularly under Negotiated Procurement-SVP.

We recommended that Management desist from adopting this practice of procuring goods and services through direct contracting and paying such by way of reimbursement to the concerned Municipal Officials and employees and instead effectively implement and conform its procurement activities as laid out in the Annual Procurement Plan. We further recommended that Management strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check which are directly paid to the suppliers, contractors, dealers and creditors.  Likewise, submit written explanation for the non-compliance of the R.A. 9184 on the above-mentioned procurements.

Lastly, it is further recommended that Management withhold the prescribed taxes in all of its procurement of goods or services in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulations.
