Accounting Office prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each class of PPE to support the PPE accounts in the General Ledger.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor appoint or designate a separate supply officer to properly manage the inventory records and reports of the Municipality in order to ascertain control and accountability of supplies and materials are in place; and
Actual physical count of inventories be conducted annually preferably at year-end, and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories which shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere with the proper presentation of Notes to FS particularly disclosures on Road Networks account based on the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for PPE accounts shall include additional required information following the format in Annex 10 provided in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point comply with the submission of GAD Plan and Budget within five (5) working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the DILG, and the corresponding Accomplishment Report shall be furnished to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We required the Accounting Office to exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of the unreconciled balances of Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts carried over from previous years and, either remit the amount or make adjustments in the books, where appropriate.

Immediate submission of copies on duly executed contracts, lease agreements and/or Memoranda of Agreement (MOA) between the Municipality and of all customers/stallholders/lessees, including their respective subsidiary ledgers for our further verification.
observations.managementAction: These contracts, lease agreements and/or Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) were duly executed and were submitted to the audit team.  Copies of which will be furnished for its compliance.

observations.recommendation: Submit to the  Audit Team the required Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY  the form prescribed in  Circular No. 2012-002; and henceforth submit to the auditors the said report within the deadline set in the said Circular.  We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the separate Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) from CY 2015 to 2019 as required in  Circular No. 2012-002 and submit the same to the audit team for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The same report was submitted to the Department of Interior & Local Government (DILG) as a requirement on the Seal of Good Local Governance. A copy of which will then be furnished to the audit team.  Required registries were already maintained by the management. The Municipal Budget Officer prepared the RAO from 2019 up to present.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth exercise with caution the formulation of the LDRRMF with emphasis on the proper computation of the total funds to be allotted.
observations.managementAction: The actual Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) received by the Municipality was the amount used as the basis for setting up the amount; thus, the deficiency.  Nonetheless, there would be no more deficiency in LDRRMF for the subsequent year as the amount used for the computation was higher than the amount of actual IRA received. The amount of understatement of LDRRMF is already considered in the 2020 utilization report. The Municipal Budget Officer committed to be more prudent in the preparation of LDRRMF appropriations.

observations.recommendation: Accounting Office continue with the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balance to come up with the details of the overdue amount and ensure that these are fully remitted to the BIR. We further recommended that henceforth a monthly reconciliation of remittances against amounts withheld be conducted to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that these aggregate unremitted amounts were the results of prior years’ operations which substantially need reconciliation.  The amounts for calendar years (CYs) 2018 & 2019 were already reconciled and remitted while the amounts remitted for CYs 2012 to 2017 are still subject of on-going reconciliation to locate the discrepancy and remit any deficiency if there be found.

observations.recommendation: Submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.
observations.managementAction: The completed contracts and purchase orders were not submitted immediately because of the lack of manpower that will focus on the preparation of the said documents.  Consequently, a particular person was hired for the sole responsibility of preparing these documents and administers the due submission of contracts to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: Closely monitor the implementation of all 20% development projects to ensure its timely completion. Also, we require management to prepare the necessary registries including Continuing Appropriations to ensure appropriateness of funds disposition. Further, we also recommended management to provide explanation as to the delayed/non-implementation of the enumerated projects cited above.
observations.managementAction: Management admitted the non-preparation of Registries of Appropriations & Obligations (RAO) for the continuing appropriations of its 20% Development Fund.  Nonetheless, management committed to comply with the recommendation in preparing such by reflecting those CY 2019 unimplemented projects in its continuing appropriations. As regards to its optimal utilization, some projects were not implemented or utilized as their nature may not qualify as a capital expenditure.  In addition, it was already pointed out during budgeting that items which are doubtful as to whether it is capital or not should no longer be included.

observations.recommendation: Revisit their Revenue Code and conduct general revision of property assessment every three (3) years to conform to Section 219 of R.A. 7160.  We further recommended that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer explained that the revision of real property assessment is a function of the Provincial Government in which the Municipality is only an implementer of such revision.  Nevertheless, the Provincial Government of Cebu is already rolling out new rates for the computation of real property taxes which shall be effective July of the current year.

observations.recommendation: Assess its existing organizational structure so that a reasonable number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job-order personnel.  We further recommended that all hired job-order personnel are covered by a Contract of Service/Job-Order.
observations.managementAction: This condition is prevalent because of the required manpower in their terminal, market & cemetery operations which can no longer be covered due to its Personal Services (PS) limitation.  Contracts of Job Order personnel were considerably complied as far back as CY 2011 when it was required by the Civil Service Commission (CSC).  However, when CSC no longer required for its submission, management also stopped requiring the execution of these contracts.  Management committed to resume the execution of Job Order contracts starting the current year 2020.

observations.recommendation: Management, through the GAD Focal Point System, submit a written justification as to the failure of its full utilization of GAD fund and henceforth fully utilize the GAD funds in accordance with the GPB.  We further recommended management to strictly follow standard procedures as laid out in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: Substantial items in the list were already disbursed particularly the medicines for mentally challenged persons and the anti-rabies vaccines.  However, there was delay in the procurement of these medicines owing to its limited nature. The management aims to optimally utilize the GAD fund as evidenced by the increase in the utilization rate of the LGU which increased to more than 90% during the year 2020.

observations.recommendation: Management should transfer the amount of ₱10,068,594.51 to the special trust fund intended for the unexpended LDRRMF from their general fund. Additionally management should allocate for the LDRRMF activities of not less than five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources to comply with Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and strictly comply with the provisions of and NDRRMC/DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction:
The management has corrected its computation for the 5% allocation for LDRRMF of the current year. Economic Enterprise revenues are already included in the base amount for allocation.

Moreover, the management has transferred the unutilized LDRRMF for CYs 2014 to 2016 in January 2020.

observations.recommendation:
Sanggunian authorize the utilization of the lump sum appropriation in accordance with the decision of the Supreme Court in the case of Hon. Gabriel Luis Quisumbing et al. vs. Hon. Gwendolyn F. Garcia, et al, GR No. 175527 promulgated on 08 December 2008.

20% Development Fund should be utilized for developmental projects that partake of the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

Prioritize expenditures on projects and programs that directly support the Philippine Development Plan, the Medium-Term Public Investment Program and the Annual Investment Program and to those provided in the DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Budget Officer has already prepared detailed budget for 20% MDF.  Moreover, the management has already minimized the charging of operating expenses against 20% MDF. Management is religiously observing the proper usage of the 20% MDF. Only those relating to infrastructure or developmental projects were charged to 20% MDF. Furthermore the management already convened and revised the 2021 appropriation  for 20%MDF clearly stipulating the specific projects for each line item

observations.recommendation: Discrepancies found in the monthly balances in the books of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be corrected and reconciled including the 2012 yearend balance for reliable financial statements presentation.
observations.managementAction: Cash in Vault balances for Trust Fund and SEF were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Disbursing Officer to see to it that the payroll be properly signed by each claimant and to require authorization if claimant is not the employee/payee concerned.  Lastly, we require management to strictly follow the documentary requirements upon the release of salaries & wages of job-order personnel as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 particularly on requiring proper authorization and identification of those individuals receiving other than the payees.
observations.managementAction: The practice of releasing payments to persons other than those reflected in the payroll was already stopped.  A system was already employed for its smooth and efficient releasing of salaries & wages to job-order personnel.

observations.recommendation: Account the under/over remittance, take appropriate actions and inform the audit team of its subsequent developments.  Moving forward, we recommended the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure timely and accurately the remittances of GSIS, PhilHealth & HDMF premiums, contributions and loan amortizations to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that the negative balance of Due to GSIS amounting ₱393,403.18 was a result of an erroneous entry which was subsequently corrected at year-end.  On the other hand, the Due to Philhealth and Pag-Ibig accounts needs further reconciliation which could either be traced as a system error of Philhealth and Pag-Ibig or an erroneous entry on the part of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property tax collection. Moreover, we advised the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an information campaign to all real property owners within the Municipality with emphasis of granting discounts when paid before due dates and the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all.  We recommended management to strictly follow standard procedures in the setting-up of RPT and SET in accordance with the provisions stated in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs so as to present a reliable and at the same time, attainable RPT and SET collectibles.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer was already advised to devise ways for the efficient collection of these receivables. Management is conducting caravan of services which includes tax collection drives, However due to the pandemic information campaign is not yet intensively conducted. The management is also working out an RPT system which is already running in the Assessor’s Office after the general revision. The treasury is set to adopt the system within the year 2021.

observations.recommendation: Submit explanation and/or legal basis on the deviation committed in the procurement of project in accordance to R.A. 9184.  We further recommended management to strictly observe the provisions in R.A. 10752 for those projects requiring acquisition of real properties.
observations.managementAction: The management explained that initially these kinds of projects were implemented by the NHA.  However, upon the President’s order, the NHA seeks assistance from the LGUs to maximize its implementation.  Nonetheless, the Terms of Reference as well as the contents and manner of bidding were practically provided by the NHA.  It was part of the set-up to have the lots be included in the contract.  By this, it is a package deal to be provided from the participating bidders.  As a requirement, the participating bidders need to have an agreement with lot owners of their intent to sell upon award of contract.

observations.recommendation: Comply with the provisions set forth in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM office was created with the appointment of some department heads that were willing to act as LDRRMO staff in a concurrent capacity.  Even so with this set-up, these department heads, acting also as LDRRMO staff, were in very good situation to effectively carry out the functions of the LDRRM office considering that they possess the authority and disposal of the Municipality’s manpower and equipment.  The recent typhoons that hit the area were a testament to this contention, wherein the LDRRM office was able to effectively carry out its responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: Compel the concerned accountable officers and immediately submit liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant and refund unexpended balances.  Otherwise, we recommended management to cause the withholding of their salaries until all advances are fully liquidated.  Henceforth, we advised management to strictly comply with laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the grant and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of the unliquidated advances is on-going for some concerned officials / employees.  Management has to make more follow-ups especially to those persons who are no longer connected with the LGU. Management targets that full liquidation of these advances will be done by end of the second quarter 2021.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC to prepare the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Procurement Monitoring Reports (PMRs) in the format prescribed and submit the same to the GPPB in accordance with Sections 7.3.5 and 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was already instructed to prepare APP using the prescribed format and have it posted in the GPPB website.  Also, BAC will prepare Procurement Monitoring Reports (PMRs) to be submitted ten (10) days after end of every semester. The BAC Secretariat is tasked to copy the data and comply with the format required. Timeframe for compliance is by end of second quarter 2021.

observations.recommendation:
All concerned officials and employees are directed to comply with the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount of excess honoraria received.

Allowable maximum rate of honoraria amounting to ₱15,500.00 originally taken from the SALINTUBIG-DILG fund should be returned and be now taken from the proceeds of the sale of bid documents and fees from contractor/supplier in the trust fund books but if there are no available funds, then the said amount will also be disallowed.

Strict compliance with the provisions of 5.3 DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005.
observations.managementAction:
Disallowed amount is for re-computation.

Municipal Accountant withheld the disallowed amount against the terminal leave benefits of three (3) retired employees.

observations.recommendation:
All concerned officials and employees are directed to comply with the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount of honoraria received.

Discontinue utilizing the 20% development fund for projects which are supposed to be charged to the general fund and should prioritize expenditures on projects and programs that directly support the Philippine Development Plan, the Medium-Term Public Investment Program and the Annual Investment Program and to those provided in the DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Strict compliance with the provisions of Section 4 (1) of P.D. No. 1445 and 6.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005.
observations.managementAction:
Disallowed amount is for re-computation. 

The Municipal Accountant withheld the amount of disallowance of the retired employees for the settlement of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant obtain complete documentation of long outstanding cash advances to support the legal claim of the Municipality from the concerned officers and employees. When deemed necessary and on valid grounds, such as for deceased accountable officers, the Municipal Accountant request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with the Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: For the deceased accountable persons, request for write-off was not prepared by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board convene regularly and cause the approval of the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality in compliance with Section 16 of Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.reason: The 10-year Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan was not yet approved by the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment accounts and the Government Equity account for the completed public infrastructure projects of ₱52.026 million in the General Fund for CY 2017 in compliance with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 so that assets and equity of the Municipality be fairly presented in the financial statement.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office failed to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the PPE and the Government Equity accounts.  First raised in CY 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: Determine/identify the public infrastructures previously transferred to the registries and comply with the target of implementation for the recognition of the Public Infrastructure of 75% by end of 2018 and 100% by end 2019 as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.reason: The 75% recognition of public infrastructures as of end of 2018 was not met by the LGU.  First raised in CY 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for the necessary accounting entry for the completed public infrastructure projects of ₱12.001 million in the Trust Fund books so that assets of the Municipality be fairly presented in the financial statement.
observations.reason: Not yet adjusted as of June 30, 2019.  First raised in CY 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: Disseminate information in every barangay regarding solid wastes segregation and disposal of wastes in the designated/temporary area and in the Final Disposal Area by conducting seminars or any other appropriate means that the management may determine.
observations.reason: The management failed to disseminate information regarding solid waste management.

observations.recommendation: Assess the necessity of wastes collection in every barangay, provide schedules of regular collections, and undertake collection of wastes using the municipality’s equipment regularly.
observations.reason: The management failed to assess necessity of wastes collection in every barangay.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor constitute a disposal committee who will appraise the supplies or properties to be disposed and conduct sale through public auction.
observations.reason: The agency failed to create a disposal committee.

observations.recommendation: Waste materials and unserviceable properties be properly disposed according to applicable rules and regulations on supply and property management in LGUs.
observations.reason: The agency failed to dispose waste materials and unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Reports (WMR) be diligently prepared, copies of which be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The agency failed to submit the IIRUP and WMR to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the trial balance and the financial statements for the MEEDO to provide adequate information on its financial condition, results of operations and its cash flows in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Section 110, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: The MEEDO has a separate bank account, however, a separate financial statements were not prepared.  First raised in CY 2015 AAR

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of San Remigio, Antique should create and pass a Municipal Ordinance on solid waste management to include items provided under Section 6 of the IRR of R.A. 9003.
observations.reason: No Municipal Ordinance on solid waste management was passed in CY 2018.  First raised in CY 2014 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare a certified computation of PS Cap applicable during the year to determine actual excess in the 45%/55% limit on personal services expenditures and determine whether PS Cost did not exceed PS Cap, otherwise, the amount expended for the PEI should be refunded by the municipal officials and employees who were recipients of the benefit.
observations.reason: Disallowed PEI is not yet fully refunded as of June 30, 2019.  First raised in CY 2009 and then in 2010 AAR

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee created by virtue of Executive Order No. 028 dated February 19, 2018 conduct the physical count of the PPE and submit the report to the Office of the Auditor. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant also conduct a reconciliation of the PPE records for fair presentation in the agency books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator in coordination with the Office of Municipal Engineer and other concerned municipal offices review the above listed unimplemented projects and consider reprogramming those projects to comply with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, and ensure regular monitoring and evaluation aimed at improving the programs/projects implementation of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the management should review and assess the necessity and validity of the above-mentioned projects that remained unimplemented as of year-end of 2019.  PAPs that are determined to be necessary for implementation be implemented in the remaining months of CY 2019 and idle funds for those that are unnecessary and not feasible for implementation in the current period due to various reasons beyond the control of the LGU be augmented or realigned to other DRRM projects in order not to deprive the public of its benefits.

In the ensuing year, the Municipal Government should consider to include in its DRRM Plan infrastructure projects that could maximize the prevention and mitigation of potential impact of a calamity and reduce vulnerabilities and exposure of communities to all hazards; and
The management should provide appropriation out of its 70% MDRRMF for PAPs related to disaster response, rehabilitation and recovery to cover all areas of disaster risk and reduction management in compliance with the NDRRMP 2011-2028 and JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that the LGU establish its GAD Database in compliance with RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women and in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 in order to come up with the most accurate and complete GAD Plan that addresses the gender issues and concerns of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 as well as the applicable provisions of Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units and COA circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.

We recommended the immediate submission of copies on duly executed contracts, lease agreements and/or Memoranda of Agreement (MOA) between the Municipality and of all customers/stallholders/lessees, including their respective subsidiary ledgers for our further verification.

We recommended that the Accounting Office continue with the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balance to come up with the details of the overdue amount and ensure that these are fully remitted to the BIR. We further recommended that henceforth a monthly reconciliation of remittances against amounts withheld be conducted to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.

We recommended management to account the under/over remittance, take appropriate actions and inform the audit team of its subsequent developments.  Moving forward, we recommended the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure timely and accurately the remittances of GSIS, PhilHealth & HDMF premiums, contributions and loan amortizations to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances.

We recommended that management submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

We recommended management to submit explanation and/or legal basis on the deviation committed in the procurement of project in accordance to R.A. 9184.  We further recommended management to strictly observe the provisions in R.A. 10752 for those projects requiring acquisition of real properties.

We recommended management to revisit their Revenue Code and conduct general revision of property assessment every three (3) years to conform to Section 219 of R.A. 7160.  We further recommended that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.

We recommended that the Municipality assess its existing organizational structure so that a reasonable number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job-order personnel.  We further recommended that all hired job-order personnel are covered by a Contract of Service/Job-Order.

We recommended management to direct the Disbursing Officer to see to it that the payroll be properly signed by each claimant and to require authorization if claimant is not the employee/payee concerned.  Lastly, we require management to strictly follow the documentary requirements upon the release of salaries & wages of job-order personnel as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 particularly on requiring proper authorization and identification of those individuals receiving other than the payees.

We recommended that management closely monitor the implementation of all 20% development projects to ensure its timely completion. Also, we require management to prepare the necessary registries including Continuing Appropriations to ensure appropriateness of funds disposition. Further, we also recommended management to provide explanation as to the delayed/non-implementation of the enumerated projects cited above.

We recommended that management submit to the COA Audit Team the required Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2019 in the form prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and henceforth submit to the auditors the said report within the deadline set in the said Circular.  We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the separate Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) from CY 2015 to 2019 as required in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and submit the same to the audit team for further evaluation.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions set forth in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that management, through the GAD Focal Point System, submit a written justification as to the failure of its full utilization of GAD fund and henceforth fully utilize the GAD funds in accordance with the GPB.  We further recommended management to strictly follow standard procedures as laid out in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee created by virtue of Executive Order No. 028 dated February 19, 2018 conduct the physical count of the PPE and submit the report to the Office of the Auditor. The Local Chief Executive cause the immediate liquidation of long outstanding cash advances of officers and employees including those who are no longer in service totaling ₱329,031.22 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. Salaries and emoluments of officers and employees with long outstanding cash advances be immediately withheld and same be applied in satisfaction of their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: First raised in CY 2008, then in 2009-2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive fill-up the mandatory positions of LDRRM Officer and the three (3) LDRRM Assistants in the LDRRM Office in accordance with NDRRMC- DILG- DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 so that comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP will be formulated and planned programs and activities of the LDRRM Office will be carried out properly and efficiently.
observations.reason: First raised in CY 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR in all procurement activities of the Municipality.
Purchase Requests or Job Requests be prepared by the requesting office early to give the BAC enough time to undertake the procurement processes.
We request that an explanation in writing by the BAC on why ITBs were published late be submitted to the Auditor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator in coordination with the Office of Municipal Engineer and other concerned municipal offices conduct regular monitoring and evaluation aimed at improving the programs/projects implementation of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: See Audit Observation No. 3, page 39-42  First raised in 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU GFPS Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group ensure that all programs, projects and activities included in the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget be judiciously monitored and implemented so that the identified gender issues for the calendar year will be addressed pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 4.1 5b (iv) and 4.1 5c (vi).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: Create the Local Council for the Protection of Children in accordance with Sec. 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 in order to advocate child rights, plan and initiate/recommend interventions and monitor children’s PPAs in the locality.
Appropriate funds to comply with the one percent requirement of Republic Act No. 9344 for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of LCPC.
Ensure that programs, projects and activities for children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 be implemented.
observations.reason: Programs, projects and activities for children were not fully implemented in CY 2019.  First raised in CY 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Budget Officer, the MDRRMO-Designate and other officials concerned be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 as well as the applicable provisions of Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units and COA circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.
observations.reason: See Audit Observation No. 9, page 54-56  First raised in CY 2012, then in 2013-2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require every official or employee to attach the above documents to their monthly claim of RATA to comply with the requirements of Local Budget Circular No. 103 and COA Circular No. 2000-005 so that the correct amount of RATA to be granted can be determined.
observations.reason: Work schedule of the SBs was not yet formulated  First raised in CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the five (5) unimplemented programs and projects included in CY 2016 DRRM Plan particularly the establishment of flood early warning system in flood prone barangays or the installation of river water level markers, and reforestation projects be immediately implemented in the ensuing year 2017 to minimize the direct impact of a potential hazard like landslide and flashfloods to people, their properties and livelihood.

Provide funds in the Municipality’s Annual Budget for solid waste management’s operational activities on capacity building of households/residents, commercial, institutional and other establishments regarding proper solid wastes segregation, wastes collections and disposal of wastes in the temporary dumpsite or in the Final Disposal Area.
The Municipal Treasurer spearhead the inventory and disposal of waste materials and unserviceable properties of the Municipality. A storeroom with a designated storeroom keeper be provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and record the depreciation of the above-enumerated depreciable assets by a debit to depreciation expense for the current year’s depreciation or a debit to prior years’ adjustments for prior years’ depreciation, and a credit to the accumulated depreciation in accordance with the guidelines set by relevant COA regulations in the computation of depreciation. We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive cause the immediate liquidation of long outstanding cash advances of officers and employees including those who are no longer in service totaling P386,464.11 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.  Salaries and emoluments of officers and employees with long outstanding cash advances be immediately withheld and same be applied in satisfaction of their unliquidated cash advances.
The Municipal Accountant obtain complete documentation of long outstanding cash advances to support the legal claim of the Municipality from the concerned officers and employees. When deemed necessary and on valid grounds, such as for deceased accountable officers, the Municipal Accountant request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with the Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit.
Provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 be strictly adhered to in the grant of cash advances to officers and employees. Management refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees who have not liquidated/settled their previous cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive fill-up the mandatory positions of LDRRM Officer and the three (3) LDRRM Assistants in the LDRRM Office in accordance with NDRRMC- DILG- DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 so that comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP will be formulated and planned programs and activities of the LDRRM Office will be carried out properly and efficiently.

We recommended the following:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR in all procurement activities of the Municipality.
Purchase Requests or Job Requests be prepared by the requesting office early to give the BAC enough time to undertake the procurement processes.
We request that an explanation in writing by the BAC on why ITBs were published late be submitted to the Auditor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator in coordination with the Office of Municipal Engineer and other concerned municipal offices conduct regular monitoring and evaluation aimed at improving the programs/projects implementation of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the LGU GFPS Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group ensure that all programs, projects and activities included in the Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget be judiciously monitored and implemented so that the identified gender issues for the calendar year will be addressed pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 4.1 5b (iv) and 4.1 5c (vi).

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board convene regularly and cause the approval of the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality in compliance with Section 16 of Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended management to compel the concerned accountable officers
and immediately submit liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant and
refund unexpended balances. Otherwise, we recommended management to cause
the withholding of their salaries until all advances are fully liquidated. Henceforth,
we advised management to strictly comply with laws, rules and regulations
pertaining to the grant and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute accumulated
depreciation in accordance with the PPSAS and existing COA issuances and make
proper adjustments in the accounts, where necessary.

We recommended management to require the Municipal Treasurer &
Municipal Accountant to follow strictly the rules and regulations provided under
the Manual on NGAS for LGUs such as the conduct of periodic physical inventory
count of supplies and the preparation & submission of the Report of Physical Count
of Inventories semestrally.
We further recommended that Municipal Accountant maintain supplies
ledger cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the stock cards
maintained in the Treasurer’s Office or the General Services Office.

We recommended that the management immediately implement the priority
projects in order not to deprive the constituents of the benefits due them and
facilitate liquidation thereof. Moreover, we recommended that the Management
update the team on the status of the said requests and based on the result of said
request, initiate the return or immediately implement the said projects so as to clear
the books of the said liability and present a reliable balance of the account.

We recommended management to intensify its tax collection drive for an
increased real property tax collection. Moreover, we advised the Municipal
Treasurer to conduct an information campaign to all real property owners within
the Municipality with emphasis of granting discounts when paid before due dates
and the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all. We recommended
management to strictly follow standard procedures in the setting-up of RPT and
SET in accordance with the provisions stated in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs so
as to present a reliable and at the same time, attainable RPT and SET collectibles.

We recommended that management submit henceforth, copies of all
perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents
for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from
execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely
auditorial and technical review thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the report and
submit as soon as possible, the AAPSI for CY 2017 AAR. Henceforth, we
recommended management strictly comply with the submission of the action plan
within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the ensuing COA Annual Audit Reports.

We recommended that management require the BAC to prepare the approved
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Procurement Monitoring Reports (PMRs) in
the format prescribed and submit the same to the GPPB in accordance with
Sections 7.3.5 and 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that henceforth, management consolidate its procurement
requirements pursuant to COA Circular 92-386 and strictly comply with the rules
and regulations on procurement under R.A. 9184.

We recommended that management require the LGU Officials concerned to
strictly comply with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated
April 13, 2011 and 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 that provides for the proper
charging of expenditure items on the 20% Development Fund in order to maximize
the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes.

We recommended the following: The Municipal Mayor constitute a disposal committee who will appraise the supplies or properties to be disposed and conduct sale through public auction. 2. Waste materials and unserviceable properties be properly disposed according to applicable rules and regulations on supply and property management in LGUs. 3. Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Reports (WMR) be diligently prepared, copies of which be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the trial balance and the financial statements for the MEEDO to provide adequate information on its financial condition, results of operations and its cash flows in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Section 110, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended the Municipality comply with the guidelines on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women set by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. The Local Chief Executive create the Municipality’s GFPS with the structure and composition provided in the Circular, and make representation with and assist the Sangguniang Bayan in the formulation of the GAD Code or ordinance. The Sangguniang Bayan prioritize the passage of the Code. Moreover, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in the Circular to be used in developing a gender-responsive GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer or with the concerned Department Head timely inform the Auditor through a letter that the appropriate signboard is already posted after award of an infrastructure project or before the start of a program/activity. We recommend that the management strictly comply with law, rules and regulations on GAD planning and implementation of activities and programs in order to address identified gender issues within the municipality.

We recommend that every RIS and PR, including purchases in times of emergency shall be accompanied by a certification from the requisitioning officer or by the duly authorized officer that the products being requisitioned and procured fall within and conform with the latest edition of the PNF Manual in accordance with the DOH AO No. 2012-0023 dated October 24, 2012. Payments should also be supported by required certifications stated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
We recommend that the Municipal officials immediately implement the projects during the year they were programmed.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare a certified computation of PS Cap applicable during the year to determine actual excess in the 45%/55% limit on personal services expenditures and determine whether PS Cost did not exceed PS Cap, otherwise, the amount expended for the PEI should be refunded by the municipal officials and employees who were recipients of the benefit.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE and submit the Report to the Office of the Auditor. Determine/identify the public infrastructures previously transferred to the registries and comply with the target of implementation for the recognition of the Public Infrastructure of 75% by end of 2018 and 100% by end 2019 as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for the necessary accounting entry for the completed public infrastructure projects of ₱12.001 million in the Trust Fund books so that assets of the Municipality be fairly presented in the financial statement.

We recommended the following:

Salaries and emoluments of officers and employees with long outstanding cash advances be immediately withheld and same be applied in satisfaction of their unliquidated cash advances.
The Municipal Accountant obtain complete documentation of long outstanding cash advances to support the legal claim of the Municipality from the concerned officers and employees. When deemed necessary and on valid grounds, such as for deceased accountable officers, the Municipal Accountant request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with the Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit.
Provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 be strictly adhered to in the grant of cash advances to officers and employees. Management refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees who have not liquidated/settled their previous cash advances.

Create the Local Council for the Protection of Children in accordance with Sec. 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 in order to advocate child rights, plan and initiate/recommend interventions and monitor children’s PPAs in the locality.
Appropriate funds to comply with the one percent requirement of Republic Act No. 9344 for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of LCPC.
Ensure that programs, projects and activities for children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 be implemented.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Budget Officer, the MDRRMO-Designate and other officials concerned be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 as well as the applicable provisions of Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units and COA circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator in coordination with the Office of Municipal Engineer and other concerned municipal offices conduct regular monitoring and evaluation aimed at improving the programs/projects implementation of the 20% Development Fund in order to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.

should ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the municipality as

for the composition of the LDDRM Office and fill up all the vacant positions and
hire additional staffs as plantilla positions required to perform the functions of

sure that the indicators for its functionality will all be present to allow proper
monitoring and evaluation in accordance with the DILG Memorandum Circular
2008-126 dated August 21, 2008

We recommend that the Management be obliged to transfer the amount of
P10,068,594.51 to the special trust fund intended for the unexpended LDRRMF
from their general fund.

activities of not less than five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular

provisions of and NDRRMC/DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1
dated March 25, 2013.

We recommend that the hiring of the job order workers be limited to
emergency or intermittent work and should not, in any case, be made to perform

therein shall be meted.

We recommend that the management should stop the practice of purchasing
any goods or services to unregistered suppliers and should see to it that all
disbursements made were supported with complete documentation.

We also recommend that the provisions in Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445, COA
Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 237 of the NIRC of the
Philippines should be strictly complied with.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat shall prepare the APP in
accordance with the prescribed revised format attached as Annex “A” per approved

We further recommend that there should be strict compliance with the
provisions of Section 4 of GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 to prevent disallowance in
audit.

and submit its resolution recommending the award to HoPE to facilitate the

provisions of Sections 37.1.1, 37.1.2, 37.1.3, and 37.1.6 of the revised IRR of R.A No.
9184 to prevent disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the BAC and BAC Secretariat review all requisitions
and see to it that brand names should not be indicated pursuant to Section 18 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that there is fair competition and not circumvent
the Procurement Law. Failure to do so will be a ground for disallowance in audit.

Provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 be strictly adhered to in the grant of cash advances to officers and employees. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Budget Officer, the LDRRMO and other officials concerned be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 as well as the applicable provisions of Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units and COA circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Government consider the following: Provide funds in the Municipality’s Annual Budget for solid waste management’s operational activities on capacity building of households/residents, commercial, institutional and other establishments regarding proper solid wastes segregation, wastes collections and disposal of wastes in the temporary dumpsite or in the Final Disposal Area. Disseminate information in every barangay regarding solid wastes segregation and disposal of wastes in the designated/temporary area and in the Final Disposal Area by conducting seminars or any other appropriate means that the management may determine. Assess the necessity of wastes collection in every barangay, provide schedules of regular collections, and undertake collection of wastes using the municipality’s equipment regularly.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and record the depreciation of the above-enumerated depreciable assets by a debit to depreciation expense for the current year’s depreciation or a debit to prior years’ adjustments for prior years’ depreciation, and a credit to the accumulated depreciation in accordance with the guidelines set by relevant COA regulations in the computation of depreciation. If there is difficulty in the determination when to commence depreciation, the Local Chief Executive shall constitute an appraisal committee to determine the carrying values and the remaining useful lives of the assets for proper computation of depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board formulate the school board budget strictly in accordance with the provisions of RA 7160 and its implementing regulations in the utilization of the SEF. Only items among the required and priority projects enumerated in the regulations be allowed to be funded out of SEF. Responsible officials cease and desist charging expenditures of the office of the district supervisor from the school board budget.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR in all procurement activities of the Municipality. We request that contracts of agreement, bidding documents, disbursement vouchers, activity accomplishment reports and other supporting documents pertaining to the above-mentioned activities, and an explanation in writing by the BAC on why ITBs were published late be submitted to the Auditor to facilitate the completion of audit.

We recommended that management cause the immediate settlement of disallowances pertaining to PEI CY 2010. Management should likewise follow up the status of appeal on disallowed PEI and monetization benefits for CY 2009 with COA Regional Office No. VI.

We recommend that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund be appropriated for projects and activities which covers the four (4) priority areas of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (2011-2028), NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of San Remigio, Antique should create and pass a Municipal Ordinance on solid waste management to include items provided under Section 6 of the IRR of R.A. 9003. We recommend that the Municipal Government always ensure that supplemental projects, programs and activities appropriated out of the savings from prior years unimplemented MDRRM Funds are accustomed to the local needs of the LGU and within the four priority areas of the NDRRM Plan in order to attain maximum benefit of the fund. We recommend that a personnel be assigned to undertake the recording of MDRRM transactions and preparation of ledgers and reports in order to ensure compliance of submission of reports to COA as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and to different concerned agencies.

We recommended that in the ensuing year, the Municipality implement programs, projects and activities embodied in the ten (10)-year Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan (2015-2025) duly approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to ensure long-term management of solid waste, in compliance with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of San Remigio, Antique should create and pass a Municipal Ordinance on solid waste management to include items provided under Section 6 of the IRR of R.A. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant secure the loan amortization schedule from the bank. A Disbursement Voucher should be prepared for every repayments and payments be made to the bank on or before every due dates to avoid automatic debit of the account. Require that the disbursements bear the approval of proper officials and supported with complete documentation. We also recommended that the accounting unit record all unrecorded prior period loan repayments in the books of account of the municipality in compliance of P.D. 1445 and New Government Accounting System Manual for Local Government Unit. Likewise, maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for loans and borrowings, and should disclose thereon all transactions, particularly releases, repayments and charges.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should convene the members of the Local Water Council to formulate specific rules and regulations on the operations of the water system to ensure that income from water system collections is fully realized to augment payment of loan amortization with the Land Bank of the Philippines.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive cause the immediate liquidation of long outstanding cash advances of officers and employees including those who are no longer in service totaling ₱496,886.11 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.  Salaries and emoluments of officers and employees with long outstanding cash advances be immediately withheld and same be applied in satisfaction of their unliquidated cash advances.
The Municipal Accountant obtain complete documentation of long outstanding cash advances to support the legal claim of the Municipality from the concerned officers and employees. When deemed necessary and on valid grounds, such as for deceased accountable officers, the Municipal Accountant request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with the Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit.
Provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 be strictly adhered to in the grant of cash advances to officers and employees. Management refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees who have not liquidated/settled their previous cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require every official or employee to attach the above documents to their monthly claim of RATA to comply with the requirements of Local Budget Circular No. 103 and COA Circular No. 2000-005 so that the correct amount of RATA to be granted can be determined.

We recommended that the five (5) unimplemented programs and projects included in CY 2016 DRRM Plan particularly the establishment of flood early warning system in flood prone barangays or the installation of river water level markers, and reforestation projects be immediately implemented in the ensuing year 2017 to minimize the direct impact of a potential hazard like landslide and flashfloods to people, their properties and livelihood.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office be created with separate funding on personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay in compliance with the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 on establishment of mandatory MDRRM Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursements be supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
Likewise, the above-mentioned disbursement for sports supplies be supported with the duly approved program design indicating therein the description and the rationale of the program, the activities to be undertaken and the materials needed with corresponding budgetary requirement, certified list of athletes and coaches, and distribution list showing the names of the athletes and coaches with their signatures that correspond to the items they received.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Budget Officer, the LDRRMO and other officials concerned be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 as well as the applicable provisions of Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units and COA circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.

We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to
comply with the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount of overtime pay.

We further recommend that there should be strict compliance with the
provisions of Section 4 (1) and (3) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommend that the amount of P4,004.00 be refunded by those persons
who caused the purchase of items in question without authority..

We also recommend that there should be strict compliance with the
provisions of Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Paragraph 9.2 of COA Circular No.
2012-001.

We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to
comply with the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount of excess honoraria
received.

We also recommend that there should be strict compliance with the
provisions of 5.3 DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 as
amended by DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-003 dated November 29, 2007.

We recommend that the Local School board limit the utilization of the Special
Education Fund to the programs and projects defined under Section 272 of RA 7160
and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 in order to maximize the benefits
that the public school students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We recommend that the Sanggunian authorize the future utilization of the
lumpsum appropriation in accordance with the decision of the Supreme Court in
the case of Hon. Gabriel Luis Quisumbing et al. vs. Hon. Gwendolyn F. Garcia, et
al, GR No. 175527 promulgated on 08 December 2008.

We also recommend that Management utilize the 20% Development Fund for
developmental projects that partake of the nature of investment or capital
expenditure.

We further recommend that Management prioritize expenditures on projects
and programs that directly support the Philippine Development Plan, the Medium-
Term Public Investment Program and the Annual Investment Program and to those
provided in the DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to
comply with the Notice of Disallowance and refund the amount of honoraria
received.

We also recommend that henceforth, Management discontinue utilizing the
20% development fund for purposes other than those allowed by DILG-DBM Joint
Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipality comply with the
provisions of Section 4 (1) of P.D. No. 1445 and 6.1 of DBM Budget Circular No.
2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 as amended by DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-003
dated November 29, 2007.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Special Education Fund is being utilized for trainings, programs and projects for the benefit of public school students and teachers.
observations.reason: Some teacher’s seminars and trainings and communication expenses were still charged to the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the cash advance voucher should have covering transmittal letter enumerating the attached documents that shall be submitted to COA. We further recommend the guidelines on the entitlement, release, use and reporting of the confidential fund as provided in DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015 be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Copies of cash advance vouchers are sometimes not submitted to COA within seven (7) days due to work constraints; guidelines being followed and submissions improved.
observations.reason: Liquidations of cash advances for confidential fund are sometimes not followed due to work constraints. Some vouchers submitted within the required timeline; follow-ups continue.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality comply with all applicable rules and regulations for its closure plan and implement protection measures by closing the open dumpsite and operate a sanitary landfill. Residual Containment Area (RCA) should be constructed and proper investments to improve solid waste management be made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Municipality has invested resources to improve its solid waste management and closure plan is being implemented.
observations.reason: Implementation of closure plan is on-going. RCA is being constructed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the SB Secretary provide Accounting Office and the Human Resource Office a copy of the report of Attendance and Absences/Resolution or Minutes of every Regular/Special Session to support the claims for RATA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 SB Secretary and Staff to furnish Accounting Office a copy of the report of attendance and regular/special session to support the claims.
observations.reason: Monitoring and provision of attendance logbook to Accounting Office is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the source of the principal amount of P2,000,000.00 be traced as to its program or purpose for its fulfillment. We also recommend to transfer the interest out of this fund to the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Verification is ongoing; interest being transferred to General Fund.
observations.reason: Verification on prior years’ transactions is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of P5,000.00 of PEI received; otherwise all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget Circular No. 2014-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Excess PEI deductions against salaries of concerned employees already implemented for some; follow-ups ongoing.
observations.reason: Settlement of disallowance thru deduction against salaries and hazard pays is ongoing for some persons.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned be directed to refund the amount in excess of P5,000.00 of PEI received; otherwise all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget Circular No. 2013-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Some deductions already made and refunds being pursued.
observations.reason: Settlement of disallowance thru deduction against salaries and officials hazard pays is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be coordinated to correct the discrepancies found in the monthly balances and that reconciliation of accounts be performed annually to achieve reliable financial statement presentation.
observations.managementAction: AAR January to June 2013 monthly discrepancies found in 2011 transactions have been reconciled; reconciliation on 2012 and 2013 is ongoing.
observations.reason: Some discrepancies found are from prior years and require further verification; reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate that the amounts representing tax collected should be remitted immediately to the BIR. Employers should not pay the taxes due out of government funds; instead withhold the said amount from salaries of officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Tax deficiencies on 2012 compensation were collected; some on-going collections for earlier years; some amounts deducted from salaries and hazard pays.
observations.reason: Some tax deficiencies collected; others still being processed and deducted from salaries/hazard pays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management send demand letters to all officials and employees concerned requiring the immediate liquidation and settlement of their cash advances. Otherwise any money claims due to these officials shall be withheld or appropriate actions instituted against them pursuant to Item 9.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Granting cash advances to officers and employees is being monitored and many have liquidated; management is following up for remaining unliquidated balances.
observations.reason: Some advances still unliquidated; follow-ups ongoing; some claimants no longer connected with LGU requiring special action.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a physical count of all properties be conducted by the inventory committee annually and a copy of the inventory report be submitted to the auditor within the time frame as required by NGAS Manual. Completed projects recorded in Construction in Progress Account should be closed and transferred to the appropriate asset accounts. The General Services Officer and the Accountant should maintain property and PPE ledger cards to facilitate reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: AAR Physical inventory is being conducted annually; asset tagging implemented; reconciliation of PPE is on-going.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of PPE account is on-going; asset tagging and annual physical count are being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to identify all completed projects and construction in progress so that proper adjustments could be affected in the books. The Municipal Engineer should submit Certificate of Completion and Acceptance on completed projects. The Municipal Accountant should prepare adjustments to transfer the completed projects to its appropriate asset account, close the asset account to Government Equity and record the asset in the Registry of Public Infrastructures.
observations.managementAction: AAR Municipal Engineer and Accountant are coordinating for verification of prior years’ records and submission of completion certificates.
observations.reason: Verification of prior years’ charges to Construction in Progress and issuance of Certificates of Completion are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to: Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of medicines under the inventory account as required under Section 114 of NGAS Manual Volume I; provide a stockroom for supplies and materials and issue them in accordance with Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual; assign a stockroom clerk to monitor and prepare a monthly report to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for adjusting journal entries.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2010 Purchased drugs and medicines are already stockroom-recorded; annual physical count on drugs, medicines and supplies conducted. Management plans to purchase a container van to be used as stockroom.
observations.reason: Management yet to purchase container van for stockroom; some supplies still being adjusted from expense to inventory.
